behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is a cross-module handoff: three source clients -> aggregation/composition -> summary input payload. A unit test of one function can miss wiring/field-drop bugs between modules (MOCK-MISS). Real OS/network is not required to reproduce this specific failure; in-process fakes for the three sources are sufficient (REAL-INFRA no). Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text is about payload completeness at the handoff, not real network/OS effects; Agentic is disqualified because this behavior does not evaluate model output, only pre-summary input assembly.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregator/composer modules with three stub source adapters returning distinct sentinel values; execute collection flow and assert the exact payload sent into summary generation contains all three sections/keys and values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience/state propagation across components: source failure -> error handling -> section annotation -> report continuation. That interaction is beyond a single pure function (MOCK-MISS). The failure can be reproduced with an in-process source adapter that raises timeout/unavailable errors; no real network/OS primitive is strictly required for this behavior (REAL-INFRA no). Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require validating real transport failure modes, only fallback behavior; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness here is fallback control flow, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run report assembly with two normal source stubs and one failing stub; assert process completes, output includes `data unavailable` for only the failed section, and script does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the quality of generated narrative content versus input facts. A stubbed summarizer would hide hallucination/omission/paraphrase errors (MOCK-HIDE). By definition this is model-output correctness (LLM-DEP yes), so Integration is too low even with deterministic assertions. Higher level rejected: Workflow is disqualified because behavior text targets a single summary generation decision, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against a fixed dataset with known key metrics/issues/deployments and assert factual fidelity criteria (key values present, no contradictions/material omissions) over multiple runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external I/O: API calls/data collection plus outbound email transport and inbox receipt verification (REAL-INFRA yes). In-process mocks can mask SMTP/API auth, TLS, provider acceptance, and delivery-path issues (MOCK-HIDE). Agentic is not the minimum for this behavior because assertion is end-to-end delivery success, not summary quality. Higher level rejected: Workflow is disqualified because behavior text does not require validating multiple agentic decisions; it requires full system delivery.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script in an environment with real reachable source endpoints (or staging equivalents) and real email delivery path; verify the target mailbox receives the report and content structure is intact for the configured recipient.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans real persistence and cross-run execution semantics: failed delivery -> filesystem save -> later run retry. This crosses process/time boundaries and OS/file behavior, and often real network failure classes (REAL-INFRA yes). Pure in-process mocks can miss path/permission/serialization/atomicity and run-to-run state issues (MOCK-HIDE). Higher levels rejected: Agentic is disqualified because correctness does not depend on model judgment; Workflow is disqualified because no multi-agentic decision chain is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger a real delivery failure (e.g., unreachable mail host), run script once and assert fallback file is created with report payload; run script again under reachable mail service and assert queued report is retried and delivered, then fallback artifact/state is cleared/marked sent.
